title: Research: Platform Architecture & Multi-Tenancy Validation
description: Validate existing Chimera architecture against latest AWS AgentCore/Strands/Bedrock capabilities. Assess multi-tenant isolation model (MicroVM, Cedar, DDB partition). Evaluate git-backed agent workspaces (EFS vs S3 vs CodeCommit). Validate team-deploy-to-own-account model where each user interacts in parallel + collaboratively. Check if the 6-table DDB design, 8-stack CDK structure, and Cognito tenant model are optimal. Output: docs/research/validation/
closeReason: Merged 3 validation docs + index from lead-arch-validation (all builders + index writer)

title: Research: Agent Collaboration & Communication Layer
description: Deep dive into inter-agent communication patterns using AWS services: SQS, EventBridge, SNS, SES, Step Functions, A2A protocol. How can agents collaborate in real-time and asynchronously? How do users collaborate THROUGH agents? Evaluate pub/sub vs queue vs event-driven patterns. Compare with OpenClaw Lane Queue model vs Strands Swarm/Graph. Research agent-to-agent notification, task handoff, shared memory, and collaborative problem-solving. Output: docs/research/collaboration/
closeReason: Merged 6 collaboration research docs + index from lead-agent-collab

title: Research: Self-Evolution, ML Experiments & Advanced Capabilities
description: Research self-improving agent capabilities: auto-skill generation, prompt A/B testing, model routing optimization. AWS infrastructure self-modification (CDK self-edit with safety rails via Cedar). ML experiment capabilities like Karpathy autoresearch pattern - agents running experiments, fine-tuning, evaluation loops. Self-expanding swarm: agents that spawn new agents. Research SageMaker, Bedrock fine-tuning, Step Functions for experiment orchestration. Output: docs/research/evolution/
closeReason: Merged 3 evolution research builders: skills/sandbox, experiments/ML, infra/swarm

title: Research: AWS Native & OSS Integration Enhancement
description: Comprehensive review of ALL AWS services that could enhance the platform beyond what existing docs cover. OSS projects: OpenSandbox, Strands, Cedar, MCP ecosystem, Playwright. Compare feature-by-feature with OpenClaw/NemoClaw - what we do better, what is missing, what is new. IaC strategy deep dive: CDK vs OpenTofu vs Pulumi, self-modifying IaC patterns. New capabilities: browser automation, code interpreter, AWS account infra control, MCP tool marketplace. Output: docs/research/integration-enhancement/
closeReason: Merged all 4 research docs + index from lead-aws-oss-integration

title: Improve lead agent workflow: merge-up, research-first, document-as-you-go
description:
Update .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md with three workflow improvements:

1. MERGE-UP CONSOLIDATION: After all builders complete and pass review, the lead must merge each builder branch into its own lead branch (git merge in worktree), then send ONE merge_ready to coordinator for the lead branch. This eliminates per-builder merges at coordinator level.

2. RESEARCH-FIRST WORKFLOW: Before implementing or documenting, leads and builders should investigate, deep-dive, analyze the problem space. Read existing code/docs, use MCP research tools, understand the landscape BEFORE writing. The scout phase exists but needs to be emphasized as mandatory for complex tasks — explore first, architect second, implement third.

3. DOCUMENT-AS-YOU-GO: As implementation proceeds, builders should document their work inline — comments where non-obvious, README updates, architecture notes. This keeps things transparent and makes it easy for other agents to pick up the work. No separate documentation phase — weave it into implementation.

File to modify: .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md
Also review: .overstory/agent-defs/builder.md, scout.md for consistency
closeReason: Merged workflow improvements to lead.md, builder.md, scout.md: merge-up consolidation, research-first, document-as-you-go, periodic rebase

title: Update lead agent hooks to allow git merge/rebase for merge-up consolidation
description: The new merge-up consolidation workflow requires leads to git merge builder branches into their lead branch and periodically git rebase on main. Current PreToolUse hooks block git merge, git rebase for lead agents. Need to update hooks to allow these operations for leads.

title: Research: Skill format compatibility — OpenClaw, Claude Code, AgentCore Gateway
description:
Deep research into skill formats across platforms and how Chimera can achieve compatibility:

1. OpenClaw SKILL.md format (v1 and v2) — what does it look like, how are skills loaded/executed?
2. Claude Code skills format — how are Claude Code skills structured (frontmatter, content, triggers)?
3. MCP tools as skills — MCP servers expose tools, can these be wrapped as Chimera skills?
4. AgentCore Gateway — does it support serving/routing skills? Can it act as a skill registry?
5. Strands tools — how does Strands define and load tools? @tool decorator pattern?
6. Compatibility layer — can Chimera natively support OpenClaw SKILL.md AND Claude Code skills AND MCP tools?
7. Skill marketplace architecture — ClawHub concept, discovery, versioning, trust tiers

Output: docs/research/skills/
closeReason: Merged skill compatibility research: 4 docs (~6600 lines) covering OpenClaw/Claude Code/MCP/Strands formats, universal adapter design, marketplace architecture
